title: MonteCarloValueAtRisk panics for non-positive simulation counts

evidence.quote:
sort.Float64s(simReturns)
	index := int(math.Floor((1 - confidence) * float64(simulations)))
...
	if index >= simulations {
		index = simulations - 1
	}
	return -simReturns[index]

varr := metrics.MonteCarloValueAtRisk(returns, 0.95, 1000)
		assert.Greater(t, varr, 0.0)
...
		varr := metrics.MonteCarloValueAtRisk(returns, 0.99, 1000)
		assert.Greater(t, varr, 0.0)

reasoning: With simulations == 0, simReturns is empty, the upper-bound clamp sets index to -1, and simReturns[-1] panics. With simulations < 0, make([]float64, simulations) panics immediately. This public library function should not crash on an invalid count without documenting that precondition.
recommendation: Validate simulations before allocating, returning 0 or an error-capable API result when simulations <= 0. Add the same style of bounds validation used for insufficient return data.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The Monte Carlo tests only pass simulations=1000 and do not cover zero or negative simulation counts.
suggestedRegressionTest: Assert MonteCarloValueAtRisk(validReturns, 0.95, 0) and MonteCarloValueAtRisk(validReturns, 0.95, -1) do not panic and return the chosen invalid-input value.
minimumFixScope: MonteCarloValueAtRisk input validation and risk tests.
